mentions.note: Consistently good with few surprises; expect solid growth quarter to drive stock higher over time.

Strong quarter: 7% revenue growth, 18% EPS growth, 10% NII growth; all four business lines beat. 2026 guidance bullish (5–7% NII growth). Sold off 4% on guilt by association—Cramer calling it innocent, best quarter of big four banks.
Barely up on Day 2; financial sector lagging expectations.
Solid performer; can do worse than solid; reports Wednesday.
Down 4% YoY, trades 12x earnings, well-run; valuation unjustifiably depressed.
Trading 11x earnings like Wells Fargo; appears cheap on surface. Cramer demands clarity on fundamentals before enthusiasm; caller Q&A highlight; cheapest stocks in market require validation.
Bank leadership on M&A/IPO bonanza ($1.2T YTD), deregulation tailwinds, and higher rates; still trading at low multiples with significant upside.
